metadata.name: Traefik Ingress Controller
metadata.description: Helm chart for Traefik v3, a modern HTTP reverse proxy and load balancer designed for microservices and Kubernetes environments.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Helm chart for Traefik v3, a modern HTTP reverse proxy and load balancer designed for microservices and Kubernetes environments.

## Prerequisites
- Decide whether this release will be the cluster's primary ingress controller before installing it.
- If you enable the dashboard ingress route, prepare the hostname, authentication middleware, and TLS secret or certificate issuer ahead of time.
- Confirm whether HTTP to HTTPS redirects, access logs, and Prometheus metrics match your cluster policy before rollout.

## Deploy
- Render `values.yaml`, review the dashboard, redirect, and metrics settings, then install or update the release with `helm upgrade --install`.
- Verify the Traefik service, entrypoints, and controller logs after the rollout completes.

## Post-Install
- Expose the dashboard only behind authentication and TLS, then verify that the intended middleware is actually attached.
- Test HTTP to HTTPS redirection and scrape the metrics endpoint if Prometheus support is enabled.
- Confirm that other ingress resources in the cluster are being reconciled by the expected Traefik instance.

## References
- [Traefik](https://traefik.io/)
- [Documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/)
- [Helm Chart](https://traefik.github.io/charts)

variables.name: dashboard_middleware
variables.title: Middleware
variables.description: Authentication middleware name for dashboard protection

variables.name: http_redirect_enabled
variables.title: HTTP Redirect
variables.description: Automatically redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS

variables.name: http_redirect_permanent
variables.title: Permanent Redirect
variables.description: Use permanent redirect (301) instead of temporary (302)
